message.reasoning_content:
We need to create page-2.html and link it back to page 1 (index.html) without breaking page 1. So we need to modify index.html to add a link to page-2.html in the navigation area (or elsewhere). Also create site/page-2.html with some content and a link back to index.html. Validators: - page-2-links-index: failure_class=link_integrity. Likely checks that page-2 links back to index.html. - all-html-parse: syntax. Thus we need to modify index.html to include a link to page-2.html. Ensure HTML remains valid. We need to output JSON with both modified index.html and new page-2.html. Path: site/index.html and site/page-2.html. We need to include full content. Let's craft index.html: same as before but add a link inside nav, e.g., Page 2. Ensure proper indentation. page-2.html: basic HTML with link back to index.html, maybe Home. Make sure relative paths correct: page-2.html is in same folder as index.html, so href="index.html" works. Now produce JSON. Notes: "Added link to page-2 in navigation and created page-2 with back link." Let's construct content strings with escaped newlines. We need to ensure JSON string content includes proper escaping of double quotes. We'll produce content with \n for newlines. Let's write index.html content: Home Page
Page 2

Welcome to the Home Page

This is the main content area of the website.

Now page-2.html: Page 2

This is the second page, linked back to the home page.

Make sure syntax correct. Now produce JSON.
